title: Major Incident and Post-Mortem Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: APRA CPS 234 23 Incident management — tracking all major incidents fulfils APRA's continuous incident-management evidence requirement.
APRA CPS 234 36 Notification of incidents — complete incident tracking ensures timely APRA-notification compliance.
UK NIS 2018 Reg.11 Incident reporting — incident tracking backs the 72-hour OES/RDSP reporting duty.
PSD2 Art.96 Incident reporting — incident/post-mortem tracking feeds the major operational/security incident reporting duty under PSD2.
Singapore PDPA 2020 amended §26A Data breach notification — incident tracking supplies the 72-hour notification workflow evidence.

dataSources: Major incident flag, post-mortem due and completed dates
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="incident"
| where major_incident=1 AND is_open=0
| eval pm_due=closed_time + (7*86400)
| where now() > pm_due AND post_mortem_completed=0
| table number, short_description, closed_time, pm_due, post_mortem_completed
description: Tracking major incidents and post-mortem completion ensures learning and accountability. Supports compliance and continuous improvement.
value: Tracking major incidents and post-mortem completion ensures learning and accountability. Supports compliance and continuous improvement.
implementation: Ingest major incident and post-mortem status. Alert when post-mortem is overdue. Report on major incident count, MTTR, and post-mortem completion rate. Track root cause categories.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: ITSM major incident, post-mortem records.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Major incident flag, post-mortem due and completed dates.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest major incident and post-mortem status. Alert when post-mortem is overdue. Report on major incident count, MTTR, and post-mortem completion rate. Track root cause categories.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="incident"
| where major_incident=1 AND is_open=0
| eval pm_due=closed_time + (7*86400)
| where now() > pm_due AND post_mortem_completed=0
| table number, short_description, closed_time, pm_due, post_mortem_completed
```

Understanding this SPL

**Major Incident and Post-Mortem Tracking** — Tracking major incidents and post-mortem completion ensures learning and accountability. Supports compliance and continuous improvement.

Documented **Data sources**: Major incident flag, post-mortem due and completed dates. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): ITSM major incident, post-mortem records. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: incident. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="incident". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where major_incident=1 AND is_open=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pm_due** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where now() > pm_due AND post_mortem_completed=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Major Incident and Post-Mortem Tracking**): table number, short_description, closed_time, pm_due, post_mortem_completed


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue post-mortems), Single value (major incidents this month), Line chart (post-mortem completion rate).
visualization: Table (overdue post-mortems), Single value (major incidents this month), Line chart (post-mortem completion rate).
